reasons: Atlas can pull enemies together, making Luo Yi's opposite-mark reaction easier to trigger.
Luo Yi adds magic burst and control after Atlas starts the grouped fight.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies first, then Luo Yi applies Yin/Yang marks to trigger reaction damage and control on the clustered targets.
worksBestWhen: Luo Yi has charges ready before Atlas engages.
Atlas catches more than one target.
failureCases: Luo Yi is too far away.
The enemy spreads or dashes before the reaction triggers.

reasons: Tigreal's grouped engage gives Luo Yi a clear target cluster for Yin-Yang Reaction.
Luo Yi can add follow-up damage and displacement after Tigreal forces enemies into one area.

proof.summary: Tigreal groups enemies, then Luo Yi follows with opposite marks to chain additional AoE control and magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal waits for Luo Yi to be in range.
Luo Yi tracks which mark state she is about to apply.
failureCases: Luo Yi uses the wrong timing or lacks charges.
The enemy uses immunity or cleanse during the setup.

reasons: Luo Yi's Diversion can help Yu Zhong enter fights from stronger flank angles.
Yu Zhong can disrupt the enemy backline while Luo Yi follows with AoE magic control.

proof.summary: Luo Yi creates a coordinated entry point, while Yu Zhong uses the flank to disrupt enemy backline positioning.
worksBestWhen: The team enters together through Diversion.
Yu Zhong starts the fight after the teleport completes.
failureCases: Yu Zhong commits before the team arrives.
Luo Yi has no follow-up spells after using Diversion.

reasons: Luo Yi can create unexpected teamfight entry angles for Claude's AoE damage window.
Luo Yi's reaction control can help keep enemies close enough for Claude to deal damage safely.

proof.summary: Luo Yi creates the entry or control window, while Claude follows with AoE marksman damage when enemies are grouped or displaced.
worksBestWhen: Claude has resources and ultimate ready before the teleport play.
Luo Yi's team enters from fog or a flank angle.
failureCases: Claude enters before the CC setup.
The enemy holds hard CC for Claude.
The teleport lands into a bad formation.

reasons: Luo Yi can create a teleport or displacement setup that gives Odette a surprise AoE channel angle.
Luo Yi's control helps keep targets near Odette's damage zone.

proof.summary: Luo Yi enables a coordinated entry or reaction setup, while Odette follows if the team can protect the channel.
worksBestWhen: The team coordinates Diversion timing clearly.
Odette casts after enemies are already controlled or distracted.
failureCases: The teleport lands into enemy counter-engage.
Luo Yi and Odette lack enough frontline protection.
